$comment: Ch 1 — THE ARC, anchor scenario (Lillard archetype). Authored from SCENARIO_SPECS_v1.md + SCENARIO_VOICE_v1.md (locked 2026-05-21). Schema mirrors bb-1-baseline.json. Textbook answer at θ=54° is v ≈ 10.36 m/s (derivation in meta.authorIntent). Per BASKETBALL_JOURNEY v2 §7, voice uses archetype + broadcast call ('BANG BANG. LOGO THREE.'), never the athlete's name.

meta.title: BANG BANG. LOGO THREE.
meta.subtitle: CH 1 — THE ARC, ANCHOR
meta.authorIntent: The pull-up from the logo. d=10.0, h_r=2.5, h_h=3.05. Textbook answer θ=54°, v=10.36 m/s. Verification: t = 10.0 / (10.36·cos 54°) = 1.642 s; y(1.642) = 2.5 + 10.36·sin(54°)·1.642 − ½·9.8·1.642² = 3.049 m — matches h_h (err 0.001 m). Pedagogically the chapter's payoff: extreme distance forces the arc lesson into its loudest form. Cultural framing is the broadcast call ('BANG BANG' from Mike Breen), never the athlete's name — per BASKETBALL_JOURNEY v2 §7.

situation.questionRevealed: Find an angle θ and velocity v that lands the ball through the hoop.

subheadVariants.1: Came up short. The legs ran out before the arc did.
subheadVariants.3+: Short again. Velocity well below threshold.

subheadVariants.1: Front iron. From 35 feet the arc has to be steeper.
subheadVariants.2: Still front iron. Lift the arc.
subheadVariants.3+: Front iron again. Push the angle up.

subheadVariants.1: Back iron. That much velocity needs a higher release.
subheadVariants.2: Still back iron. Cut the velocity.

subheadVariants.1: Long over the rim. Distance was misjudged.
subheadVariants.3+: Far overshoot. Velocity well above threshold.

subheadVariants.1: Air ball from deep. The release fell apart.
subheadVariants.2: Air ball again. Aim for the rim.
subheadVariants.3+: Still air ball. Check angle and velocity.

ghostArc.description: Textbook answer θ=54°, v=10.36 m/s. The arc crests over 6 meters above the floor — visibly the highest shot in the curriculum and an audible 'whoa' moment when the ghost arc renders. Confirms the chapter insight: the deepest threes are the highest arcs.

hints.body: The shot is far, but the equations don't change. Projectile motion still.

Two equations, two unknowns:
  x(t) = v·cos(θ)·t
  y(t) = h_r + v·sin(θ)·t − ½·g·t²
Distance is much bigger; expect a much bigger velocity.
Eliminate t with t = d/(v·cos θ). A high arc helps — try θ near 54°. Solve for v.

workedSteps: Solve y(t) = h_h for v → v ≈ 10.36 m/s.

intro.headline: BANG BANG. LOGO THREE.
intro.subhead: Thirty-five feet. The highest arc anyone takes.

subheadByFlavor.SWISH: Clean release from the logo. Net dies.
subheadByFlavor.GLASS: Caught a slice of square on the way down.
subheadByFlavor.RIM_DROP: Sat on the iron, then fell.

miss.headline: OFF THE MARK

diagnosticByCategory.FRONT_RIM: Front iron. From 35 feet the arc has to be steeper.
diagnosticByCategory.BACK_RIM: Back iron. That much velocity needs a higher release.
diagnosticByCategory.SHORT: Came up short. The legs ran out before the arc did.
diagnosticByCategory.OVERSHOOT: Long over the rim. Distance was misjudged.
diagnosticByCategory.AIRBALL: Air ball from deep. The release fell apart.

bracketHintByCategory.FRONT_RIM: Geometry: θ near 54°, push v near 10.4.
bracketHintByCategory.BACK_RIM: Geometry: θ near 54°, cut v by ~0.5.
bracketHintByCategory.SHORT: Geometry: θ near 54°, push v near 10.4.
bracketHintByCategory.OVERSHOOT: Geometry: θ near 54°, cut v by ~0.8.

miss.afterAllHintsCopy: All hints revealed. The math is on the table.
